arts.ttl: Genoa manager Gilardino rallies team ahead of Bologna clash
arts.sum: As Serie A action resumes following the international break, Genoa manager Alberto Gilardino addressed the media to preview his side’s upcoming match against Bologna. The former Italian striker struck a defiant tone, emphasizing his belief in the squad despite recent struggles and a growing injury list. Gilardino began by praising the work ethic of the players who remained with the club during the break. “With the players I had available these two weeks, between those with national teams and those here, it has been weeks of excellent work and attitude from the boys. arts.ttl: Serie A | Genoa v Bologna: Match Preview
arts.sum: The Stadio Luigi Ferraris will host a pivotal match between two struggling Rossoblu sides this weekend as Genoa welcomes Bologna in a Serie A encounter that could have significant implications for both teams’ seasons. Genoa, under increasing pressure, find themselves in the relegation zone after a dismal run of form. Their recent 5-1 thrashing at the hands of Atalanta BC highlighted the team’s defensive frailties, having conceded a league-high 15 goals this season. Manager Alberto Gilardino faces mounting scrutiny, with his side having lost four of their last five league fixtures. arts.ttl: Genoa President Zangrillo: “Attacking Gilardino is disgraceful, we will recover”
arts.sum: Genoa president Alberto Zangrillo, has spoken out about the club’s current difficulties, addressing issues ranging from the team’s performance to rumors of a potential sale. In a candid interview with La Gazzetta dello Sport, Zangrillo defended manager Alberto Gilardino and called for unity within the organization. Regarding the team’s challenging start to the season, Zangrillo stated, “Yes, I expected it because anything can happen in football, and looking at the calendar, I had taken it into account.
